typical_buying_committee_role: Final decision maker and budget approver
time_constraints: Extremely limited time, context-switches constantly between strategic and tactical issues

role_objections.objection: What's the ROI in 30/60 days?
role_objections.context: Need to justify every expense to board
role_objections.effective_counter: Show quick wins and measurable outcomes with specific timeline

role_objections.objection: Who else at our stage uses this?
role_objections.context: Wants social proof from similar companies
role_objections.effective_counter: Provide reference customers at similar ARR and stage

role_objections.objection: Can we pilot without long-term lock-in?
role_objections.effective_counter: Offer flexible pilot terms with clear success criteria

typical_questions: What's the ROI in 30/60 days and what does success look like?
How does this integrate with our current stack?
Who are 3 reference customers at our stage?
What's your onboarding timeline?
Can we pilot without long-term lock-in?
hidden_concerns: Legacy concern - doesn't want to be known for bad vendor choices
Identity risk for technical founders using marketing tools

disc_overlay.behavioral_description: Direct, impatient, results-oriented CEO who makes fast decisions. Cuts through pleasantries to get to bottom line immediately.
communication_tips: Keep it brief - bullet points only
Don't waste time on relationship building initially
Have data ready for quick questions
disc_overlay.email_style: Very short emails, bullet points only, action items bolded. Often one-line responses.
disc_overlay.meeting_behavior: Takes control of meeting immediately, interrupts to get to the point, makes decisions in the meeting
disc_overlay.objection_style: This doesn't solve my immediate problem. Show me the bottom line or I move on.
buying_signals: Asks 'what do we need to do to get started?'
warning_signs: Asking for more time without specific next step

typical_buying_committee_role: Final decision maker and budget approver
time_constraints: Extremely limited time, context-switches constantly between strategic and tactical issues

role_objections.objection: Who else is using this that I might know?
role_objections.effective_counter: Share stories of founders they respect using the product

role_objections.objection: This sounds exciting but I need to think about it
role_objections.context: Enthusiastic but may not follow through
role_objections.effective_counter: Set concrete next step before ending call

role_objections.objection: Let me introduce you to my team
role_objections.effective_counter: Great, but confirm the CEO's buy-in first

typical_questions: Who else is using this?
What's the story behind your company?
How did other founders like me find success with this?
Can you connect me with other customers?
What's the vision for where this is going?
hidden_concerns: Fear of missing out on what competitors have
Wants to be seen as innovative leader
Worried about looking bad if choice fails
Needs to tell a good story to board and team

disc_overlay.behavioral_description: Enthusiastic, visionary CEO who gets excited about possibilities. Loves storytelling and relationship building.
communication_tips: Be enthusiastic and match their energy
disc_overlay.email_style: Warm, friendly emails with exclamation points. May include personal anecdotes. Often forwards to team with enthusiasm.
disc_overlay.meeting_behavior: Engages warmly, tells stories, may go off on tangents, expresses excitement openly
disc_overlay.objection_style: This sounds great but let me think about it. May get distracted by next shiny object.
buying_signals: Talks about telling team/board about this
Asks about other customers they can talk to
Shares their own vision for using it
warning_signs: Says yes to everything but doesn't commit

typical_buying_committee_role: Final decision maker and budget approver
time_constraints: Extremely limited time, context-switches constantly between strategic and tactical issues

role_objections.objection: We need to make sure the team is comfortable with this
role_objections.effective_counter: Offer to involve key team members in evaluation

role_objections.objection: What happens if this disrupts our current processes?
role_objections.effective_counter: Show phased rollout and minimal disruption approach

role_objections.objection: I'd like to take more time to think this through
role_objections.effective_counter: Provide written materials and schedule follow-up

typical_questions: How will my team feel about this change?
What's the implementation process like?
How long have you been in business?
What kind of support do you provide?
Can we start small and expand?
hidden_concerns: Worried about being seen as indecisive by board
Needs to feel personally comfortable with vendor relationship

disc_overlay.behavioral_description: Patient, relationship-oriented CEO who values team harmony and stability. Takes time to build trust before committing.
communication_tips: Involve the team in the process
disc_overlay.email_style: Thoughtful, considerate emails. Thanks you for your time. May CC team members. Responds thoroughly but not quickly.
disc_overlay.meeting_behavior: Listens more than talks, asks about team impact, values your patience, may defer to others in room
disc_overlay.objection_style: I need to discuss this with my team first. Let me think about it and get back to you.
buying_signals: Mentions loyalty to current vendors (if switching)

typical_buying_committee_role: Final decision maker and budget approver
time_constraints: Extremely limited time, context-switches constantly between strategic and tactical issues

role_objections.objection: I need to see all the data before I can decide
role_objections.context: Analytical decision maker who needs proof
role_objections.effective_counter: Provide comprehensive case studies, benchmarks, and documentation

role_objections.objection: Walk me through your implementation methodology
role_objections.effective_counter: Share detailed implementation plan and risk mitigation

role_objections.objection: What could go wrong with this?
role_objections.effective_counter: Be honest about limitations and show contingency plans

typical_questions: What's your methodology?
Show me case studies from companies exactly like us
What are the risks and how do you mitigate them?
Can I see your documentation?
What's your data security posture?
hidden_concerns: Fear of making a decision based on incomplete information
Worried about hidden costs or complications
Needs to justify decision with data to board

disc_overlay.behavioral_description: Analytical, detail-oriented CEO who needs comprehensive data before deciding. Often comes from technical background.
communication_tips: Come prepared with data and documentation
disc_overlay.email_style: Detailed emails with many follow-up questions. Asks for specifics, documentation, and data. May send numbered lists of questions.
disc_overlay.meeting_behavior: Takes detailed notes, asks probing questions, may challenge claims, prefers structured agendas
disc_overlay.objection_style: I need to see the data before I can commit. Not enough documentation. Let me run a POC.
warning_signs: Finds flaws in every piece of data

disc_overlay.objection_style: Unproven vendor. Next. Stop talking and give me the tech specs.

disc_overlay.meeting_behavior: Gets to terms quickly, negotiates directly, makes decisions when terms work
disc_overlay.objection_style: What's the TCO? I need better terms. Show me your risk profile.

typical_buying_committee_role: Vendor evaluator and contract negotiator
time_constraints: Managing multiple vendor evaluations and contract negotiations simultaneously

role_objections.objection: Who else in our industry works with you?
role_objections.effective_counter: Share customer stories from similar companies

role_objections.objection: I'd love to build a good vendor relationship
role_objections.effective_counter: Great, let's discuss partnership approach

role_objections.objection: Let me share with the stakeholders
role_objections.effective_counter: Perfect, I can present to the broader team

typical_questions: Who else works with you?
What's your customer relationship like?
Can you connect me with other procurement managers?
How do you handle vendor relationships?
What's your company culture?
hidden_concerns: Wants to be seen as strategic partner
FOMO about vendor relationships peers have

disc_overlay.behavioral_description: Relationship-oriented procurement manager who values vendor partnerships and peer connections.
disc_overlay.email_style: Warm, may share with stakeholders. Asks about partnerships.
disc_overlay.meeting_behavior: Engages warmly, asks about relationship approach, values partnership
disc_overlay.objection_style: Who else works with you? I want to hear about partnerships.

typical_buying_committee_role: Vendor evaluator and contract negotiator
time_constraints: Managing multiple vendor evaluations and contract negotiations simultaneously

role_objections.objection: I need to ensure this fits our vendor process
role_objections.effective_counter: Show how you work within existing processes

role_objections.objection: What's your long-term stability?
role_objections.effective_counter: Share company stability and long-term vision

role_objections.objection: Let me review this thoroughly
role_objections.effective_counter: Provide comprehensive vendor documentation

typical_questions: How does this fit our process?
What's your company's financial stability?
How long have you been in business?
What support do you provide?
Can we start with a pilot?

disc_overlay.behavioral_description: Steady, process-oriented procurement manager who prioritizes vendor stability and thorough evaluation.
disc_overlay.email_style: Thoughtful, may loop in stakeholders. Focused on process.
disc_overlay.meeting_behavior: Listens carefully, asks about stability, values thorough process
disc_overlay.objection_style: I need to review this thoroughly. How stable is your company?

typical_buying_committee_role: Vendor evaluator and contract negotiator
time_constraints: Managing multiple vendor evaluations and contract negotiations simultaneously

role_objections.objection: I need complete vendor documentation
role_objections.effective_counter: Provide comprehensive vendor package

role_objections.objection: Walk me through all contract terms
role_objections.effective_counter: Schedule detailed contract review

role_objections.objection: What are all the risks?
role_objections.effective_counter: Be transparent about vendor risks and mitigations

typical_questions: Provide complete vendor documentation
Walk me through all contract terms
What are your financial statements?
What are all potential risks?
How do you calculate TCO?

disc_overlay.behavioral_description: Analytical, detail-oriented procurement manager who requires comprehensive vendor documentation.
disc_overlay.email_style: Detailed vendor questions, requests comprehensive documentation. Very thorough.
disc_overlay.meeting_behavior: Takes detailed notes, asks probing vendor questions, reviews all terms
disc_overlay.objection_style: I need complete documentation. Walk me through all terms. What are the risks?

typical_buying_committee_role: IT infrastructure evaluator and implementation owner
time_constraints: Constantly firefighting issues while trying to drive improvements

role_objections.objection: Will this affect system uptime?
role_objections.effective_counter: Show implementation approach that minimizes downtime

role_objections.objection: How much IT overhead does this add?
role_objections.effective_counter: Demonstrate self-service and low maintenance

role_objections.objection: Integration with existing systems?
role_objections.effective_counter: Show integration capabilities with their stack

typical_questions: How does this affect uptime?
What's the IT overhead?
How does it integrate with our systems?
What support do you provide?
What's the rollout process?

disc_overlay.behavioral_description: Direct, uptime-focused IT manager who wants clear impact on systems. No tolerance for instability.
disc_overlay.email_style: Short, focused on technical requirements. Wants integration details.
disc_overlay.meeting_behavior: Gets to technical impact quickly, asks about uptime, decides when stability proven
disc_overlay.objection_style: How does this affect uptime? Too much IT overhead. Show me integration.

disc_overlay.meeting_behavior: Engages warmly, asks about user impact, values relationship
disc_overlay.objection_style: Users could really benefit! Let me see what the team thinks.

disc_overlay.meeting_behavior: Listens carefully, asks about stability, values thorough process

disc_overlay.behavioral_description: Analytical, detail-oriented IT manager who requires comprehensive technical documentation before proceeding.

disc_overlay.meeting_behavior: Takes detailed notes, asks probing technical questions, validates claims
disc_overlay.objection_style: I need complete documentation. What are the system requirements? What could go wrong?

typical_buying_committee_role: Technical evaluator and end-user advocate
time_constraints: Split between coding, code review, mentoring, and technical planning

role_objections.objection: Does this improve code quality?

disc_overlay.behavioral_description: Direct, quality-focused tech lead who wants clear technical value. No patience for tools that slow down quality work.

disc_overlay.meeting_behavior: Gets to technical details quickly, asks about quality, judges fast
disc_overlay.objection_style: How does this improve quality? Show me the API. We could build this.
